steps.title: Analyze what happens to the mean when removing 78.2°F
steps.content:
Reading from the histogram, the 11 temperatures consist of 1 value in [40, 45), 2 in [45, 50), 3 in [50, 55), 3 in [55, 60), 1 in [60, 65), and 1 value of 78.2°F.

To determine how the mean changes, I'll compare 78.2°F to the original mean. Using the interval midpoints as estimates: approximately $1(42.5) + 2(47.5) + 3(52.5) + 3(57.5) + 1(62.5) + 78.2 \approx 608$, giving a mean around $608 \div 11 \approx 55.3°F$.

Even at the extremes:
  • Minimum possible mean: $(40 + 90 + 150 + 165 + 60 + 78.2) \div 11 \approx 53°F$
  • Maximum possible mean: approaching $(45 + 100 + 165 + 180 + 65 + 78.2) \div 11 \approx 57.6°F$
Since 78.2°F exceeds the original mean in all cases, removing it must decrease the mean. Statement I is always true.

steps.title: Analyze what happens to the median when removing 78.2°F
steps.content:
In the original 11-value dataset, the median is the 6th value (middle position). From the histogram, positions 4, 5, and 6 all fall in [50, 55).

After removing 78.2°F (which is at position 11), the new 10-value dataset has positions 1–10. The new median is the average of the 5th and 6th values: both still in [50, 55).

Let the three [50, 55) values be $a \leq b \leq c$:
  • Original median = $c$ (the 6th position)
  • New median = $\frac{a + b}{2}$ (average of 5th and 6th)
The new median is less than the original only if $a < c$. However, the histogram allows $a = b$ (the 5th and 6th values could be equal, e.g., both 52.5°F). If $a = b$, then new median $= \frac{a + a}{2} = a$, which may equal $c$. Statement II is not necessarily true.

steps.title: Identify which statement(s) must be true
steps.content:
Statement I is always true: removing a value greater than the mean always decreases the mean. Since 78.2°F exceeds the original mean in every possible scenario, the new mean must be less than the original.

Statement II is not necessarily true: the 5th and 6th values (both in [50, 55)) could be equal, making the new median equal to the original median, not less. The histogram does not guarantee they differ.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
